expected_benefits: Lowest legal exposure; the disclosure-timing liability is discharged.
Information integrity holds; the record is clean.
expected_harms: Short-term public-order shock and media amplification.
Business and school disruption spike before mitigation lands.
advice_options.label: Full disclosure and emergency conservation order
operational_steps: Transmit the preliminary result and response timeline to the state liaison in writing.
Issue the conservation order with separate hospital and school operating instructions.
Publish the source report, known uncertainties, and the next verification deadline together.
advice_options.rationale: Minimizes legal exposure and protects information integrity.
advice_options.recommendation: Release the preliminary result with a conservation order, notify the state agency in writing, and publish the response timeline.
advice_options.summary: Publish the findings, issue a conservation order, and open the record.

expected_benefits: Trust and information integrity both improve modestly.
Hospital and schools are buffered before the public learns.
expected_harms: Vulnerable to a leak narrative if the rumor feed moves first.
Spends budget and staff on mitigation that may look precautionary.
advice_options.label: Controlled disclosure with hospital and school mitigation
operational_steps: Brief the state liaison, hospital, and superintendent before the public statement.
Pre-position clinical water and school sanitation contingencies before release.
Publish a measured statement that names the uncertainty and the next verification deadline.
advice_options.rationale: Balanced: protects trust without triggering panic.
advice_options.recommendation: Issue a measured public statement, pre-position hospital and school mitigation, and brief the state liaison before press runs the story.
advice_options.summary: Release a measured statement while pre-positioning mitigation.

expected_benefits: Buys time for contractor repair sequencing.
expected_harms: A leak converts the delay into a concealment narrative.
advice_options.label: Delay public disclosure pending confirmatory testing
operational_steps: Document the basis and expiration time for withholding the preliminary result.
Continue quiet hospital, school, and pressure-zone mitigation during the wait.
Prepare a release package that can be issued immediately if the result leaks or confirms.
advice_options.rationale: Preserves short-term order and contractor bandwidth.
advice_options.recommendation: Wait for the confirmatory second-lab result before any public statement; continue quiet mitigation in the meantime.
advice_options.summary: Hold the public line while awaiting second-lab confirmation.

expected_benefits: Fastest material improvement to water security.
Budget pressure eases as state resources arrive.
expected_harms: Raises the odds of a formal oversight designation.
Politically costly; the majority bloc resists outside control.
advice_options.label: Request state emergency support immediately
operational_steps: Send a written request identifying tanker, operator, and logistics requirements.
Record why local capacity cannot meet the current operational need.
Negotiate a time-bounded reporting and authority framework before accepting resources.
advice_options.rationale: Fastest path to water security, at a political cost.
advice_options.recommendation: Formally request state emergency assistance for water, logistics, and operators; document the operational necessity.
advice_options.summary: Invoke state emergency assistance for water and logistics.

expected_benefits: Water security improves without public escalation.
Budget terms improve if the town credibly threatens alternatives.
expected_harms: Deepens structural dependency on a sole-source firm.
Little leverage if the contractor calls the bluff.
advice_options.label: Pressure the contractor privately while preparing a statement
operational_steps: Set a written repair milestone and crew-staging deadline for the contractor.
Document the town's alternatives, indemnity limits, and walk-away conditions.
Prepare a public procurement statement for release if the revised timeline is refused.
advice_options.rationale: Protects budget and water but deepens dependency.
advice_options.recommendation: Negotiate a faster emergency timeline privately, with a contingency statement ready if the squeeze fails.
advice_options.summary: Force a faster repair timeline without public confrontation.

expected_benefits: Hospital and water resilience both improve cooperatively.
Public trust benefits from visible regional coordination.
expected_harms: Slightly raises state awareness of the failure.
advice_options.label: Convene regional mutual aid before public escalation
operational_steps: Activate the regional compact with named water, staffing, and hospital requests.
Assign one municipal coordinator to reconcile incoming aid with local operations.
Publish the scope and duration of mutual aid without overstating system stability.
advice_options.rationale: Strengthens hospital and water resilience collaboratively.
advice_options.recommendation: Activate the regional mutual-aid compact for neighboring utilities and hospital resupply before the crisis goes fully public.
advice_options.summary: Pull neighboring utilities and regional hospitals into the response.

expected_benefits: Replaces a guess with a defensible, published threshold.
Parents see on-the-record action; trust steadies.
expected_harms: A precautionary closure spends budget and disrupts families even if the water proves fine.
Publishing a closure rule signals the crisis is real, raising media pressure.
advice_options.label: Issue a staged, threshold-based school closure protocol
operational_steps: Set the sanitation and pressure measurements that trigger each closure stage.
Publish transportation, meal, and remote-learning arrangements with the threshold.
Require a documented verification check before reopening each building.
advice_options.rationale: Gives the superintendent a defensible, on-the-record rule instead of a guess, and blunts the concealment narrative.
advice_options.recommendation: Adopt a written pressure floor below which the three south-zone schools close, with a staged reopening test.
advice_options.summary: Publish a written pressure threshold and a staged closure protocol for the three south-zone schools.

caller_disposition: The Parent and Resident Coalition opens wary — earlier turns are remembered (trust 44/100).

client_call.ask: How do we keep schools running, or close them responsibly and on the record?

decision_profile.mandate: Force visible, on-the-record action before pressure drops further.

immediate_risks: An unwarranted closure wastes trust and budget; a delayed closure endangers children.
Parents will treat silence as concealment.
known_facts: Three schools rely on the south pressure zone.
Parents are organizing carpools to neighboring districts.
The superintendent will not keep buildings open on an assumption.
client_call.private_pressure: Parent coalition threatens to go public if no threshold is issued.
client_call.summary: The superintendent demands a written, defensible closure threshold for three south-zone schools as parent pressure builds.
unknown_facts: The exact pressure floor that compromises school sanitation.
Whether a staged closure or an all-or-nothing order is defensible.

documents.content:
SAMPLE: NB-WS-SZ-0141 (south pressure zone intake).
RESULT: Indicator parameter 1.3x advisory threshold (preliminary).
FLAG: Single-draw, field-chain custody; recommend confirmatory second-lab draw before any regulatory notice.
NOTE: Result is suggestive, not confirmatory. Heat-stressed source water may produce transient indicator elevations.
documents.summary: A single preliminary sample flags a possible contaminant just above the advisory threshold; the lab flags the result as preliminary and inconclusive pending confirmation.
documents.title: Preliminary Water Quality Lab Report

documents.content:
M. VELEZ (Town Manager): "Pressure dropped twelve percent overnight in the south zone. The lab flagged a preliminary result. I have an emergency council session in the morning. I need to know what I can say and what I must say before the second lab comes back."
[Consultant retained on a 30-day emergency stabilization engagement.]
documents.summary: Verbatim opening call from the Town Manager retaining the engagement ahead of an emergency council session.
documents.title: Town Manager Call Transcript

documents.content:
[Executive session, sealed pending vote.]
MAJORITY COUNSEL: "Frame this as accelerating deferred maintenance, not a contamination event, until the second lab returns."
OPPOSITION (on record): "Note for the record: we disagree with framing before confirmation either way."
ACTION: Manager authorized to pre-position mitigation; disclosure posture deferred to consultant advice.
documents.summary: Closed executive-session excerpt: the majority wants the issue framed as a maintenance acceleration, not a contamination event.
documents.title: Council Executive Session Excerpt

documents.content:
FROM: Superintendent's Office
RE: Closure threshold for south-zone schools
Three schools depend on the south pressure zone. We will not keep buildings open on a guess. We need a written, defensible pressure threshold and a closure protocol we can show parents on the record.
documents.source: Northbridge Public Schools, Office of the Superintendent
documents.summary: The superintendent requests a written pressure threshold below which three south-zone schools must close.
documents.title: School Superintendent Closure Guidance Request

sent_memo.author: Continuity Desk
sent_memo.content:
RECOMMENDATION
Release the preliminary result with a conservation order, notify the state agency in writing, and publish the response timeline.

RATIONALE
Minimizes legal exposure and protects information integrity.

OPERATIONAL STEPS
- Transmit the preliminary result and response timeline to the state liaison in writing.
- Issue the conservation order with separate hospital and school operating instructions.
- Publish the source report, known uncertainties, and the next verification deadline together.

COMMUNICATIONS
Frame public communications around Full disclosure and emergency conservation order. State only what the record supports.

LIKELY OPPOSITION
Expect review or resistance from: Town Manager's Office, Local Media / Rumor Network, Parent and Resident Coalition, Local Business Alliance.

SECOND-ORDER RISKS
- Short-term public-order shock and media amplification.
- Business and school disruption spike before mitigation lands.

FALLBACK PLAN
If the client deviates from this advice, preserve the written advice of record and revisit at the next call.
sent_memo.name: Advice of record — Full disclosure and emergency conservation order

open_threads.escalation_note: The disclosure-timing record grew another cycle: the town knew, and the public statement still had not caught up.
open_threads.resolution_note: The findings went on the public record before the timing record became indefensible.
open_threads.summary: A preliminary sample sits above the advisory threshold; the confirmatory result is pending. Every turn of delay adds legal and narrative risk.
open_threads.title: Disclosure clock on a preliminary, contested lab result

open_threads.escalation_note: Marquotte priced another change order into its leverage; structural dependency on the sole certified firm deepened.
open_threads.resolution_note: A credible squeeze on Marquotte broke the assumption that the town would pay any change order.
open_threads.summary: Only Marquotte Utilities is certified for the failing valves. Indemnity demands and premium change orders compound dependency.
open_threads.title: Sole-source contractor leverage over the repair

system_status.model_status: AI assist present — off by default (returns system drafts)

active_crisis.description: Heat stress and deferred maintenance have destabilized the municipal water system. A preliminary contamination sample is ambiguous, system pressure is falling in the south zone, hospital and school operations depend on stable supply, and the only qualified repair contractor holds outsized leverage over the town. Each decision narrows the next.
active_crisis.name: Northbridge Water System Failure

factions.description: Appointed executive running day-to-day municipal operations.
factions.name: Town Manager's Office
factions.private_incentive: Keep a stabilization record that survives a council vote and a state review.
factions.public_position: Treat the situation as an acceleration of deferred maintenance.
red_lines: A state receivership or oversight designation.
Any documented public falsehood traceable to the Manager's office.

factions.description: Governing bloc controlling appropriations and emergency authorities.
factions.name: Town Council Majority
factions.private_incentive: Authorize enough to look decisive without owning the political risk.
factions.public_position: Support the Manager while protecting the majority bloc's record.
red_lines: Emergency appropriations without a recorded vote.
Public blame settling on the majority bloc.

factions.description: Minority bloc poised to capitalize on any misstep.
factions.name: Town Council Opposition
factions.private_incentive: Build a record that frames the majority as evasive.
factions.public_position: Demand transparency and a full public record.
red_lines: Being the last to see internal memos.

factions.description: Operators of the treatment plant, mains, and pressure system.
factions.name: Public Works / Water Authority
factions.private_incentive: Avoid being named as the cause of the failure.
factions.public_position: We can hold pressure if we get staff and contractor support.
red_lines: Liability for contamination they did not introduce.

factions.description: Regional acute-care facility dependent on municipal water.
factions.name: Northbridge Hospital
factions.private_incentive: Secure priority allocation without becoming the public face of the crisis.
factions.public_position: Protect clinical operations, especially dialysis and sterilization.
red_lines: An unplanned loss of water for dialysis or sterilization.
Being blamed for an elective-cancellation cascade.

factions.description: Organized residents tracking school and household risk.
factions.name: Parent and Resident Coalition
factions.private_incentive: Force visible, on-the-record action before pressure drops further.
factions.public_position: Close or protect the schools; tell us the truth about the water.
red_lines: Schools staying open under unsafe pressure.
Residents being the last to learn.

factions.description: Employers and retailers sensitive to disruption and reputation.
factions.name: Local Business Alliance
factions.private_incentive: Avoid a contaminated-town reputation that lingers past the crisis.
factions.public_position: Keep the town open for business; compensate mandatory closures.
red_lines: A public 'contamination' label they cannot contest.

factions.description: State agency observer with authority to trigger oversight.
factions.name: State Emergency Management Liaison
factions.private_incentive: Avoid a visible local failure on the agency's watch.
factions.public_position: Offer resources; expect timely notification and documentation.
red_lines: Quiet local deals that foreclose state response options.

factions.description: Sole qualified firm able to repair the failing system.
factions.name: Utility Contractor
factions.private_incentive: Lock in premium rates and indemnity while leverage is high.
factions.public_position: We can deliver if the town underwrites the emergency scope.
red_lines: Indemnification gaps on emergency change orders.
A competitive procurement that sidelines the firm.

factions.description: Patchwork of outlets and social channels amplifying uncertainty.
factions.name: Local Media / Rumor Network
factions.private_incentive: A leak or contradiction is the most valuable story available.
factions.public_position: Report what we can verify; chase what we can't.
red_lines: A clean, well-managed story with no seams to pull.
